---
title: "Creating pipelines"
description: "How OpenPipeline composes components into VDSL3 pipelines."
order: 30
---

A workflow chains components into a pipeline. OpenPipeline workflows are
**VDSL3 pipelines** — built with [Viash](https://viash.io) from a
`config.vsh.yaml` plus a `main.nf`. Creating one is a Viash task, so **refer to
the [Viash website](https://viash.io/guide/) for the how-to** (declaring
`dependencies`, the `nextflow_script` resource, `fromState` / `toState` wiring,
`runIf`, building, …). This page covers only the conventions specific to
OpenPipeline.

## OpenPipeline conventions

- **Layout.** A workflow lives in `src/workflows/<category>/<name>/` with a
  `config.vsh.yaml`, `main.nf` (a `run_wf` workflow), a `nextflow.config`, a
  `test.nf`, and — by convention — an `integration_test.sh` for local
  integration testing (see [Running tests](running-tests.qmd)). Categories follow
  the analysis stage (`rna`, `integration`, `annotation`, `multiomics`, …).
- **State-based channels.** Data flows as `[id, state]` tuples; components read
  from the state with `fromState` and write back with `toState`. The lens behind
  this — single-sample → multi-sample → merged → integrated — is explained in
  [the data-state view](../concepts/data-state.qmd).
- **Declare dependencies.** Every component or sub-workflow used in `main.nf`
  must be listed under `dependencies` in the config; Viash resolves and includes
  the compiled modules at build time (imports are not written by hand).
- **Dataflow utilities.** Reuse `dataflow/concatenate_h5mu`, `split_modalities`,
  `merge`, and `split_h5mu` for common channel manipulations rather than
  reimplementing them.
- **Resource labels.** Assign CPU/memory via labels on each component; the
  workflow inherits them. See [Tune compute resources](../guides/tune-resources.qmd).

## Build and run locally

Use `viash ns build` to build a workflow and its dependencies locally for
development, then run it with `nextflow run`. See [Running tests](running-tests.qmd).
Published packages are built and hosted by Viash Hub
([packages.viash-hub.com](https://packages.viash-hub.com)).

**Next:** [Running tests →](running-tests.qmd)
